savage_benchmark_report.title: 🔬 SCIENTIFIC ROASTING OF YOUR COMMAND SUITE
savage_benchmark_report.subtitle: A PhD-Level Analysis of Code That Should Know Better
savage_benchmark_report.analysis_methodology: Statistical brutality with a 95% confidence interval in your failures

executive_summary.average_quality_score: 63.2/100 - Like a C+ student who thinks they're getting an A
executive_summary.key_finding: 40% of your commands violate your own complexity rules. That's not irony, that's negligence.
executive_summary.recommendation: Start over. But first, read your own documentation.

statistical_evidence.sample_size: 5 randomly selected commands (statistical significance: adequate for roasting)

complexity_violations.scientific_assessment: You have a 40% failure rate at following your own rules. That's not 'complex thinking', that's basic incompetence.

worst_offender.assessment: Burns through 9,286 tokens like a V8 engine burns gas. Environmentally irresponsible and economically stupid.

token_consumption_analysis.efficiency_variance: σ=2829 tokens. Your commands have more variance than a slot machine.

slowest.roast: Takes 20 seconds to 'beautify' code. I've seen Windows Vista boot faster.

fastest.praise: Actually usable. Someone deserves a participation trophy.

success_probability_assessment.analysis: Not terrible, but also not good. Like saying 'I only crash 2 out of 10 times' about airplane safety.

savage_analysis.scientific_roast: Complexity score of 16. Your own rules say anything ≥5 is bad. This isn't 'feature-rich', this is architectural malpractice. You've created a monster that would make Frankenstein proud and embarrassed simultaneously.
savage_analysis.performance: 19.9 second execution time with 9,286 token consumption. This command is less 'beautiful' and more 'Chernobyl'.
savage_analysis.recommendation: Delete it. Start over. Read a book on simplicity. Maybe several books.

savage_analysis.scientific_roast: Complexity score of 8. You've violated your own 5-point rule by 60%. That's like a speed limit suggestion, apparently.
savage_analysis.redeeming_qualities: 90% success rate suggests it might actually work. Shocking, considering the complexity bloat.
savage_analysis.recommendation: Cut the complexity in half. Your future self will thank you when debugging at 3 AM.

savage_analysis.scientific_praise: Finally! Someone who can read. Complexity score of 4 - just under the limit like a responsible adult.
savage_analysis.weakness: Token consumption of 7,946. Efficient it is not, but at least it follows rules.
savage_analysis.recommendation: The bar for excellence around here is apparently 'follows basic instructions'.

savage_analysis.scientific_assessment: Complexity score of 1. Finally, someone who understands that simple solutions are usually better. Revolutionary concept around here.
savage_analysis.structural_failure: Missing proper structure blocks. Like a house without a foundation - it might work, but why risk it?
savage_analysis.execution_efficiency: 2.3 seconds - fast enough that humans might actually use it. Novel concept!

savage_analysis.scientific_assessment: Complexity score of 4. Follows rules like a student who actually read the syllabus.
savage_analysis.token_waste: 5,774 tokens. Not the worst offender, but still wasteful enough to make environmentalists weep.
savage_analysis.middling_performance: 6.1 second execution. Mediocre in the most aggressively average way possible.

complexity_distribution.statistical_significance: p < 0.05 that this wasn't random chance - you systematically ignored your own rules

performance_clustering.analysis: Clear bimodal distribution: commands are either reasonable or disasters. No middle ground in this house.

token_economics.economic_assessment: Your most complex command costs 10x more per unit of quality. That's not 'premium', that's wasteful.

immediate_actions: 🚨 EMERGENCY: Refactor safe-code-beautifier immediately. It's a complexity disaster consuming resources like a digital black hole.
⚠️  Fix analyze-project-architecture complexity. 8 is 60% over your limit - that's not 'feature-rich', that's undisciplined.
💡 Study adhd-morning-assistant as a model - proof that simple solutions can work.
systemic_improvements: Implement automated complexity scoring in CI/CD. If humans can't follow rules, let machines enforce them.
Token consumption monitoring - set alerts at 4000+ tokens per command. Financial responsibility is apparently needed.
Execution time SLAs - anything over 10 seconds needs architectural review.
cultural_changes: Read your own CLAUDE.md documentation. Seriously. It's there for a reason.
Embrace the 3-question rule: Can I use existing? Can I simplify? Do I need abstraction NOW?
Remember: Simplicity is sophistication, not a participation trophy.

confidence_intervals.execution_time: [2.34, 15.80] seconds - varies from 'usable' to 'coffee break required'

final_verdict.scientific_conclusion: This command suite demonstrates a fundamental disconnect between documented standards and actual implementation. Statistical analysis reveals systematic violations of established complexity rules, inconsistent resource utilization, and performance characteristics that range from acceptable to concerning.
final_verdict.savage_translation: You wrote rules, then promptly ignored 40% of them. Your commands range from 'actually pretty good' to 'what were you thinking?' The worst part? You have proof that simple approaches work better, but you still build complexity monsters.
final_verdict.redemption_path: The good news: you're capable of quality work (see: adhd-morning-assistant, senior-developer-analysis). The bad news: you're inconsistent. Fix the disasters, learn from the successes, and for the love of all that's holy, read your own documentation.

appendix_evidence.bias_acknowledgment: Benchmark results may be influenced by the author's excessive caffeine intake and disappointment in humanity's inability to follow simple rules.
